chunks.text: Ana Claudia Arias is a Professor and EE Division Chair at UC Berkeley. She holds a PhD in Physics from the University of Cambridge (2001), and MS and BS degrees in Physics from the Federal University of Paraná in Brazil (1997, 1995). She joined UC Berkeley in January 2011. Previously, she was Manager of Printed Electronic Devices at PARC (Xerox) and led the semiconductor group at Plastic Logic in Cambridge, UK.

Her research focuses on flexible electronic systems using solution-processed electronic materials. She employs printing techniques to fabricate flexible large-area electronic devices and sensors. Her research areas include Physical Electronics (PHY), Power and Energy (ENE), and Flexible and Printed Electronics. She is associated with research centers such as Berkeley Emerging Technologies Research (BETR) and Berkeley Wireless Research Center (BWRC).

Selected publications include work on all-organic optoelectronic sensors for pulse oximetry (Nature Communications, 2014), flexible lithium-ion batteries (Advanced Energy Materials, 2014), all-printed flexible organic transistors (Advanced Materials, 2014), and device modeling of organic photovoltaics (Journal of Applied Physics, 2013). Other publications cover ink-jet printed photosensors, materials for large area electronics, and stability studies of organic transistors.

Honors and awards include IEEE Fellow (2025), National Academy of Inventors Fellow (2025), Tampere University Honorary Doctorate (2022), NAE Gilbreth Lectureship (2017), FLEXI R&D Achievements Award (2017), and Bakar Fellows Spark Award (2014).
